mainEntity.name: Should we handle answer engine optimization ourselves or hire a specialist, and how do we decide?

acceptedAnswer.text:
If you’re deciding between DIY answer engine optimization and hiring external help, consider your team’s capacity for both editorial work and technical implementation. AEO is less about tricks and more about systematic, high-quality Q&A content.

You can often start in-house, then bring in specialists once you hit platform or schema complexity limits.

Key factors:
- Internal skills in content strategy, writing, and basic markup
- Volume of questions and topics you need to cover
- Need for advanced measurement, multi-engine monitoring, or custom integrations
- Budget tolerance for ongoing, not just one-off, support

Practically, begin by having your team refactor a small set of pages and track AI citations. If you see promise but lack time or technical depth, that’s a good moment to explore outside support.

This staged approach lets you validate the value of AEO before committing to larger investments.
acceptedAnswer.description: Start by assessing your team’s ability to write strong Q&A content and add basic schema. Try a small in-house pilot and track whether AI citations improve. If you see potential but lack time or technical depth, that’s the right moment to consider hiring a specialist for ongoing AEO support.
